tEn: MineDojo: Building Open-Ended Embodied Agents with Internet-Scale Knowledge
tZh: MineDojo：利用互联网规模知识构建开放式具身智能体
sEn: MineDojo integrates internet-scale knowledge with Minecraft to train generalist embodied agents on thousands of open-ended tasks.
sZh: MineDojo将互联网规模的知识与Minecraft游戏相结合，训练通用具身智能体完成数千种开放式任务。
absEn: Autonomous agents have made great strides in specialist domains like Atari games and Go. However, they typically learn tabula rasa in isolated environments with limited and manually conceived objectives, thus failing to generalize across a wide spectrum of tasks and capabilities. Inspired by how humans continually learn and adapt in the open world, we advocate a trinity of ingredients for building generalist agents: 1) an environment that supports a multitude of tasks and goals, 2) a large-scale database of multimodal knowledge, and 3) a flexible and scalable agent architecture. We introduce MineDojo, a new framework built on the popular Minecraft game that features a simulation suite with thousands of diverse open-ended tasks and an internet-scale knowledge base with Minecraft videos, tutorials, wiki pages, and forum discussions. Using MineDojo's data, we propose a novel agent learning algorithm that leverages large pre-trained video-language models as a learned reward function. Our agent is able to solve a variety of open-ended tasks specified in free-form language without any manually designed dense shaping reward. We open-source the simulation suite, knowledge bases, algorithm implementation, and pretrained models (https://minedojo.org) to promote research towards the goal of generally capable embodied agents.
absZh: 自主智能体在Atari游戏和围棋等专业领域取得了巨大进步。然而，它们通常在孤立环境中从零开始学习，目标有限且由人工设计，因此难以泛化到广泛的任务和能力。受人类在开放世界中持续学习和适应的启发，我们主张构建通用智能体的三个要素：1）支持多种任务和目标的模拟环境，2）大规模多模态知识数据库，3）灵活可扩展的智能体架构。我们提出了MineDojo，这是一个基于热门游戏Minecraft的新框架，包含一个具有数千种多样化开放式任务的模拟套件，以及一个包含Minecraft视频、教程、维基页面和论坛讨论的互联网规模知识库。利用MineDojo的数据，我们提出了一种新颖的智能体学习算法，该算法利用大型预训练视频-语言模型作为学习到的奖励函数。我们的智能体能够解决各种由自由形式语言指定的开放式任务，无需任何手动设计的密集塑形奖励。我们开源了模拟套件、知识库、算法实现和预训练模型（https://minedojo.org），以推动实现通用具身智能体目标的研究。

contrib.en: Introduces MineDojo, a framework with thousands of open-ended tasks in Minecraft.
contrib.zh: 提出MineDojo框架，包含数千个Minecraft中的开放式任务。

contrib.en: Provides an internet-scale knowledge base of 730K+ YouTube videos, 6K+ Wiki pages, and 340K+ Reddit posts.
contrib.zh: 提供互联网规模的知识库，包括73万+ YouTube视频、6千+ Wiki页面和34万+ Reddit帖子。

contrib.en: Proposes MineCLIP, a contrastive video-language model trained on YouTube data for reward learning.
contrib.zh: 提出MineCLIP，一种在YouTube数据上训练的对比视频-语言模型，用于奖励学习。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that MineCLIP-based agents achieve competitive performance to hand-engineered rewards on 12 tasks.
contrib.zh: 证明基于MineCLIP的智能体在12个任务上达到与手工设计奖励相当的性能。

contrib.en: Introduces a novel evaluation protocol for creative tasks using a learned video-language model.
contrib.zh: 引入一种使用学习到的视频-语言模型评估创造性任务的新协议。

contrib.en: Open-sources simulation suite, knowledge bases, algorithms, and pretrained models to promote research.
contrib.zh: 开源模拟套件、知识库、算法和预训练模型以促进研究。

limits.en: Agent learning is evaluated on only 12 tasks, limiting generalizability.
limits.zh: 智能体学习仅在12个任务上评估，限制了泛化性。

limits.en: MineCLIP reward model may not generalize to tasks outside Minecraft domain.
limits.zh: MineCLIP奖励模型可能无法泛化到Minecraft领域之外的任务。

limits.en: Creative task evaluation relies on a learned metric, which may not perfectly align with human judgment.
limits.zh: 创造性任务评估依赖学习到的指标，可能不完全与人类判断一致。

limits.en: The framework is specific to Minecraft, limiting applicability to other embodied environments.
limits.zh: 该框架特定于Minecraft，限制了在其他具身环境中的适用性。

limits.en: Computational overhead of MineCLIP requires optimization techniques for practical RL training.
limits.zh: MineCLIP的计算开销需要优化技术以实现实际的强化学习训练。

items.en: Autonomous agents have made great strides in specialist domains like Atari games and Go. However, they typically learn tabula rasa in isolated environments with limited and manually conceived objectives, thus failing to generalize across a wide spectrum of tasks and capabilities. Inspired by how humans continually learn and adapt in the open world, we advocate a trinity of ingredients for building generalist agents: 1) an environment that supports a multitude of tasks and goals, 2) a large-scale database of multimodal knowledge, and 3) a flexible and scalable agent architecture. We introduce MineDojo, a new framework built on the popular Minecraft game that features a simulation suite with thousands of diverse open-ended tasks and an internet-scale knowledge base with Minecraft videos, tutorials, wiki pages, and forum discussions. Using MineDojo’s data, we propose a novel agent learning algorithm that leverages large pre-trained video-language models as a learned reward function. Our agent is able to solve a variety of open-ended tasks specified in free-form language without any manually designed dense shaping reward. We open-source the simulation suite, knowledge bases, algorithm implementation, and pretrained models (https://minedojo.org) to promote research towards the goal of generally capable embodied agents.
items.zh: 自主智能体在雅达利游戏和围棋等专业领域取得了巨大进展。然而，它们通常在孤立的环境中从头学习，目标有限且人为设计，因此无法在广泛的任务和能力上泛化。受人类在开放世界中持续学习和适应的启发，我们主张构建通用智能体的三个要素：1）支持多种任务和目标的環境，2）大规模多模态知识数据库，3）灵活且可扩展的智能体架构。我们介绍了MineDojo，这是一个基于流行游戏Minecraft构建的新框架，拥有包含数千个多样化开放式任务的模拟套件，以及包含Minecraft视频、教程、维基页面和论坛讨论的互联网规模知识库。利用MineDojo的数据，我们提出了一种新颖的智能体学习算法，该算法利用大型预训练视频语言模型作为学习到的奖励函数。我们的智能体能够解决以自由形式语言指定的各种开放式任务，无需任何手动设计的密集塑形奖励。我们开源了模拟套件、知识库、算法实现和预训练模型（https://minedojo.org），以促进朝着通用具身智能体目标的研究。

items.capEn: Figure 1: MineDojo is a novel framework for developing open-ended, generally capable agents that can learn and adapt continually to new goals. MineDojo features a benchmarking suite with thousands of diverse open-ended tasks specified in natural language prompts, and also provides an internet-scale, multimodal knowledge base of YouTube videos, Wiki pages, and Reddit posts. The database captures the collective experience and wisdom of millions of Minecraft gamers for an AI agent to learn from. Best viewed zoomed in.
items.capZh: 图1：MineDojo是一个新颖的框架，用于开发开放式、通用能力的智能体，能够持续学习和适应新目标。MineDojo包含一个基准测试套件，其中有数千个以自然语言提示指定的多样化开放式任务，并提供了一个互联网规模的多模态知识库，包括YouTube视频、维基页面和Reddit帖子。该数据库捕捉了数百万Minecraft游戏玩家的集体经验和智慧，供AI智能体学习。建议放大查看。

items.en: Developing autonomous embodied agents that can attain human-level performance across a wide spectrum of tasks has been a long-standing goal for AI research. There has been impressive progress towards this goal, most notably in games [80, 85, 126] and robotics [68, 99, 146, 134, 107]. These embodied agents are typically trained tabula rasa in isolated worlds with limited complexity and diversity. Although highly performant, they are specialist models that do not generalize beyond a narrow set of tasks. In contrast, humans inhabit an infinitely rich reality, continuously learn from and adapt to a wide variety of open-ended tasks, and are able to leverage large amount of prior knowledge from their own experiences as well as others.
items.zh: 开发能够在广泛任务中达到人类水平的自主具身智能体一直是AI研究的长期目标。在这方面已经取得了令人瞩目的进展，尤其是在游戏[80, 85, 126]和机器人[68, 99, 146, 134, 107]领域。这些具身智能体通常在孤立的世界中从头训练，复杂性和多样性有限。尽管性能很高，但它们是专业模型，无法泛化到狭窄任务集之外。相比之下，人类生活在无限丰富的现实中，不断从广泛多样的开放式任务中学习和适应，并能够利用来自自身经验和他人的大量先验知识。

items.en: We argue that three main pillars are necessary for generalist embodied agents to emerge. First, the environment in which the agent acts needs to enable an unlimited variety of open-ended goals [116, 71, 120, 117]. Natural evolution is able to nurture an ever-expanding tree of diverse life forms thanks to the infinitely varied ecological settings that the Earth supports [117, 129]. This process has not stagnated for billions of years. In contrast, today’s agent training algorithms cease to make new progress after convergence in narrow environments [80, 146]. Second, a large-scale database of prior knowledge is necessary to facilitate learning in open-ended settings. Just as humans frequently learn from the internet, agents should also be able to harvest practical knowledge encoded in large amounts of video demos [42, 77], multimedia tutorials [79], and forum discussions [127, 65, 54]. In a complex world, it would be extremely inefficient for an agent to learn everything from scratch through trial and error. Third, the agent’s architecture needs to be flexible enough to pursue any task in open-ended environments, and scalable enough to convert large-scale knowledge sources into actionable insights [19, 96]. This motivates the design of an agent that has a unified observation/action space, conditions on natural language task prompts, and adopts the Transformer pre-training paradigm [27, 91, 15] to internalize knowledge effectively.
items.zh: 我们认为，通用具身智能体的出现需要三个主要支柱。首先，智能体行动的环境需要支持无限多样的开放式目标[116, 71, 120, 117]。自然进化能够培育出不断扩展的多样化生命形式，得益于地球支持的无限变化的生态设置[117, 129]。这一过程数十亿年来从未停滞。相比之下，当今的智能体训练算法在狭窄环境中收敛后便不再取得新进展[80, 146]。其次，需要大规模的先验知识数据库来促进开放式环境中的学习。正如人类经常从互联网学习一样，智能体也应该能够从大量视频演示[42, 77]、多媒体教程[79]和论坛讨论[127, 65, 54]中获取实用知识。在复杂世界中，智能体通过试错从头学习一切将极其低效。第三，智能体的架构需要足够灵活以在开放式环境中追求任何任务，并且足够可扩展以将大规模知识源转化为可操作的见解[19, 96]。这促使设计一种具有统一观察/动作空间、以自然语言任务提示为条件并采用Transformer预训练范式[27, 91, 15]来有效内化知识的智能体。

items.en: In light of these three pillars, we introduce MineDojo, a new framework to help the community develop open-ended, generally-capable agents. It is built on the popular Minecraft game, where a player explores a procedurally generated 3D world with diverse types of terrains to roam, materials to mine, tools to craft, structures to build, and wonders to discover. Unlike most other games [80, 85, 126], Minecraft defines no specific reward to maximize and no fixed storyline to follow, making it well suited for developing open-ended environments for embodied AI research. We make the following three major contributions:
items.zh: 鉴于这三个支柱，我们介绍了MineDojo，这是一个帮助社区开发开放式、通用能力智能体的新框架。它基于流行的Minecraft游戏构建，玩家探索程序生成的3D世界，其中有多种地形可供漫游、材料可挖掘、工具可制作、结构可建造以及奇迹可发现。与大多数其他游戏[80, 85, 126]不同，Minecraft没有定义要最大化的特定奖励或要遵循的固定故事情节，使其非常适合为具身AI研究开发开放式环境。我们做出以下三个主要贡献：

items.en: MineDojo provides convenient APIs on top of Minecraft that standardize task specification, world settings, and agent’s observation/action spaces. We introduce a benchmark suite that consists of thousands of natural language-prompted tasks, making it two orders of magnitude larger than prior Minecraft benchmarks like the MineRL Challenge [48, 62]. The suite includes long-horizon, open-ended tasks that cannot be easily evaluated through automated procedures, such as “build an epic modern house with two floors and a swimming pool”. Inspired by the Inception score [98] and FID score [55] that are commonly used to assess AI-generated image quality, we introduce a novel agent evaluation protocol using a large video-language model pre-trained on Minecraft YouTube videos. This complements human scoring [104] that is precise but more expensive. Our learned evaluation metric has good agreement with human judgment in a subset of the full task suite considered in the experiments.
items.zh: MineDojo在Minecraft之上提供了便捷的API，标准化了任务规范、世界设置以及智能体的观察/动作空间。我们引入了一个基准测试套件，包含数千个自然语言提示的任务，比之前的Minecraft基准测试（如MineRL挑战赛[48, 62]）大两个数量级。该套件包括难以通过自动化程序评估的长时域开放式任务，例如“建造一栋带有两层楼和游泳池的史诗级现代房屋”。受常用于评估AI生成图像质量的Inception分数[98]和FID分数[55]的启发，我们引入了一种新颖的智能体评估协议，使用在Minecraft YouTube视频上预训练的大型视频语言模型。这补充了精确但更昂贵的人工评分[104]。我们学习到的评估指标在实验考虑的部分任务套件中与人类判断具有良好的一致性。

items.en: Minecraft has more than 100 million active players [131], who have collectively generated an enormous wealth of data. They record tutorial videos, stream live play sessions, compile recipes, and discuss tips and tricks on forums. MineDojo features a massive collection of 730K+ YouTube videos with time-aligned transcripts, 6K+ free-form Wiki pages, and 340K+ Reddit posts with multimedia contents (Fig. 3). We hope that this enormous knowledge base can help the agent acquire diverse skills, develop complex strategies, discover interesting objectives, and learn actionable representations automatically.
items.zh: Minecraft拥有超过1亿活跃玩家[131]，他们共同产生了海量数据。他们录制教程视频、直播游戏过程、整理配方并在论坛上讨论技巧和窍门。MineDojo拥有庞大的收藏，包括73万多个带有时序对齐字幕的YouTube视频、6千多个自由格式的维基页面以及34万多个带有多媒体内容的Reddit帖子（图3）。我们希望这个庞大的知识库能帮助智能体自动获取多样技能、发展复杂策略、发现有趣目标并学习可操作的表示。

items.en: We develop a new learning algorithm for embodied agents that makes use of the internet-scale domain knowledge we have collected from the web. Using the massive volume of YouTube videos from MineDojo, we train a video-text contrastive model in the spirit of CLIP [92], which associates natural language subtitles with their time-aligned video segments. We demonstrate that this learned correlation score can be used effectively as an open-vocabulary, massively multi-task reward function for RL training. Our agent solves the majority of 12 tasks in our experiment using the learned reward model (Fig. 2). It achieves competitive performance to agents trained with meticulously engineered dense-shaping rewards, and in some cases outperforms them, with up to 73% improvement in success rates. For open-ended tasks that do not have a simple success criterion, our agents also perform well without any special modifications.
items.zh: 我们开发了一种新的具身智能体学习算法，该算法利用了从网络收集的互联网规模领域知识。利用MineDojo的大量YouTube视频，我们训练了一个视频文本对比模型，其精神类似于CLIP[92]，将自然语言字幕与其时序对齐的视频片段关联起来。我们证明，这种学习到的相关性分数可以有效地用作RL训练的开放词汇、大规模多任务奖励函数。我们的智能体使用学习到的奖励模型解决了实验中12个任务中的大部分（图2）。其性能与使用精心设计的密集塑形奖励训练的智能体相当，并且在某些情况下优于它们，成功率提升高达73%。对于没有简单成功标准的开放式任务，我们的智能体无需任何特殊修改也能表现良好。

items.en: In summary, this paper proposes an open-ended task suite, internet-scale domain knowledge, and agent learning with recent advances on large pre-trained models [13]. We have open-sourced MineDojo’s simulator, knowledge bases, algorithm implementations, pretrained model checkpoints, and task curation tools at https://minedojo.org/. We hope that MineDojo will serve as an effective starter framework for the community to develop new algorithms and advance towards generally capable embodied agents.
items.zh: 总之，本文提出了一个开放式任务套件、互联网规模领域知识以及利用大型预训练模型[13]最新进展的智能体学习。我们已在https://minedojo.org/开源了MineDojo的模拟器、知识库、算法实现、预训练模型检查点和任务整理工具。我们希望MineDojo能成为社区开发新算法并朝着通用具身智能体迈进的有效入门框架。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Visualization of our agent’s learned behaviors on four selected tasks. Leftmost texts are the task prompts used in training. Best viewed on a color display.
items.capZh: 图2：我们的智能体在四个选定任务上的学习行为可视化。左侧文本是训练中使用的任务提示。建议在彩色显示器上查看。

items.en: MineDojo offers a set of simulator APIs help researchers develop generally capable, open-ended agents in Minecraft. It builds upon the open-source MineRL codebase [48] and makes the following upgrades: 1) We provide unified observation and action spaces across all tasks, facilitating the development of multi-task and continually learning agents that can constantly adapt to new scenarios and novel tasks. This deviates from the MineRL Challenge design that tailors observation and action spaces to individual tasks; 2) Our simulation unlocks all three types of worlds in Minecraft, including the Overworld, the Nether, and the End, which substantially expands the possible task space, while MineRL only supports the Overworld natively; and 3) We provide convenient APIs to configure initial conditions and world settings to standardize our tasks.
items.zh: MineDojo提供了一套模拟器API，帮助研究人员在Minecraft中开发通用能力、开放式的智能体。它基于开源的MineRL代码库[48]构建，并进行了以下升级：1）我们为所有任务提供了统一的观察和动作空间，促进了多任务和持续学习智能体的开发，使其能够不断适应新场景和新任务。这与MineRL挑战赛的设计不同，后者为个别任务定制观察和动作空间；2）我们的模拟解锁了Minecraft中的所有三种世界类型，包括主世界、下界和末地，这大大扩展了可能的任务空间，而MineRL仅原生支持主世界；3）我们提供了便捷的API来配置初始条件和世界设置，以标准化我们的任务。

items.en: With this MineDojo simulator, we define thousands of benchmarking tasks, which are divided into two categories: 1) Programmatic tasks that can be automatically assessed based on the ground-truth simulator states; and 2) Creative tasks that do not have well-defined or easily-automated success criteria, which motivates our novel evaluation protocol using a learned model (Sec. 4). To scale up the number of Creative tasks, we mine ideas from YouTube tutorials and use OpenAI’s GPT-3 [15] service to generate substantially more task definitions. Compared to Creative tasks, Programmatic tasks are simpler to get started, but tend to have restricted scope, limited language variations, and less open-endedness in general.
items.zh: 借助这个MineDojo模拟器，我们定义了数千个基准测试任务，分为两类：1）程序化任务，可根据真实模拟器状态自动评估；2）创意任务，没有明确定义或易于自动化的成功标准，这促使我们使用学习到的模型进行新颖的评估协议（第4节）。为了扩大创意任务的数量，我们从YouTube教程中挖掘想法，并使用OpenAI的GPT-3[15]服务生成更多任务定义。与创意任务相比，程序化任务更容易上手，但通常范围受限、语言变化有限且开放性较低。

items.en: We formalize each programmatic task as a 5-tuple: T=(G,𝒢,ℐ,f𝒮,fℛ)𝑇𝐺𝒢ℐsubscript𝑓𝒮subscript𝑓ℛT=(G,\mathcal{G},\mathcal{I},f_{\mathcal{S}},f_{\mathcal{R}}). G𝐺G is an English description of the task goal, such as “find material and craft a gold pickaxe”. 𝒢𝒢\mathcal{G} is a natural language guidance that provides helpful hints, recipes, or advice to the agent. We leverage OpenAI’s GPT-3-davinci API to automatically generate detailed guidance for a subset of the tasks. For the example goal “bring a pig into Nether”, GPT-3 returns: 1) Find a pig in the overworld; 2) Right-click on the pig with a lead; 3) Right-click on the Nether Portal with the lead and pig selected; 4) The pig will be pulled through the portal! ℐℐ\mathcal{I} is the initial conditions of the agent and the world, such as the initial inventory, spawn terrain, and weather. f𝒮subscript𝑓𝒮f_{\mathcal{S}}: st→{0,1}→subscript𝑠𝑡01s_{t}\rightarrow\{0,1\} is the success criterion, a deterministic function that maps the current world state stsubscript𝑠𝑡s_{t} to a Boolean success label. fℛsubscript𝑓ℛf_{\mathcal{R}}: st→ℝ→subscript𝑠𝑡ℝs_{t}\rightarrow\mathbb{R} is an optional dense reward function. We only provide fℛsubscript𝑓ℛf_{\mathcal{R}} for a small subset of the tasks in MineDojo due to the high costs of meticulously crafting dense rewards. For our current agent implementation (Sec. 4.1), we do not use detailed guidance. Inspired by concurrent works SayCan [3] and Socratic Models [143], one potential idea is to feed each step in the guidance to our learned reward model sequentially so that it becomes a stagewise reward function for a complex multi-stage task.
items.zh: 我们将每个程序化任务形式化为一个五元组：T=(G,𝒢,ℐ,f𝒮,fℛ)。G是任务目标的英文描述，例如“找到材料并制作一把金镐”。𝒢是自然语言指导，为智能体提供有用的提示、配方或建议。我们利用OpenAI的GPT-3-davinci API自动为部分任务生成详细指导。对于示例目标“将猪带入下界”，GPT-3返回：1）在主世界找到一头猪；2）用拴绳右键点击猪；3）右键点击下界传送门，同时选中拴绳和猪；4）猪将被拉过传送门！ℐ是智能体和世界的初始条件，例如初始库存、生成地形和天气。f𝒮: st→{0,1}是成功标准，一个确定性函数，将当前世界状态st映射到布尔成功标签。fℛ: st→ℝ是可选的密集奖励函数。由于精心制作密集奖励的成本很高，我们仅为MineDojo中一小部分任务提供fℛ。对于我们当前的智能体实现（第4.1节），我们不使用详细指导。受同期工作SayCan[3]和Socratic Models[143]的启发，一个潜在的想法是将指导中的每一步依次输入到我们学习到的奖励模型中，使其成为复杂多阶段任务的分阶段奖励函数。

items.en: MineDojo provides 4 categories of programmatic tasks with 1,581 template-generated natural language goals to evaluate the agent’s different capabilities systematically and comprehensively:
items.zh: MineDojo提供4类程序化任务，包含1,581个模板生成的自然语言目标，以系统全面地评估智能体的不同能力：

items.en: Survival: surviving for a designated number of days.
items.zh: 生存：生存指定天数。

items.en: Harvest: finding, obtaining, cultivating, or manufacturing hundreds of materials and objects.
items.zh: 收获：寻找、获取、培育或制造数百种材料和物品。

items.en: Tech Tree: crafting and using a hierarchy of tools.
items.zh: 科技树：制作和使用一系列工具。

items.en: Combat: fighting various monsters and creatures that require fast reflex and martial skills.
items.zh: 战斗：与需要快速反应和战斗技能的各种怪物和生物战斗。

items.en: Each task template has a number of variations based on the terrain, initial inventory, quantity, etc., which form a flexible spectrum of difficulty. In comparison, the NeurIPS MineRL Diamond challenge [48] is a subset of our programmatic task suite, defined by the task goal “obtain 1 diamond" in MineDojo.
items.zh: 每个任务模板根据地形、初始库存、数量等有多种变体，形成灵活的难度谱系。相比之下，NeurIPS MineRL钻石挑战赛[48]是我们程序化任务套件的一个子集，由任务目标“获得1颗钻石”定义。

items.en: We define each creative task as a 3-tuple, T=(G,𝒢,ℐ)𝑇𝐺𝒢ℐT=(G,\mathcal{G},\mathcal{I}), which differs from programmatic tasks due to the lack of straightforward success criteria. Inspired by model-based metrics like the Inception score [98] and FID score [55] for image generation, we design a novel task evaluation metric based on a pre-trained contrastive video-language model (Sec. 4.1). In the experiments, we find that the learned metric exhibits a high level of agreement with human evaluations (see Table 2).
items.zh: 我们将每个创意任务定义为一个三元组：T=(G,𝒢,ℐ)，与程序化任务不同，因为缺乏直接的成功标准。受图像生成中基于模型的指标（如Inception分数[98]和FID分数[55]）的启发，我们设计了一种基于预训练对比视频语言模型的新颖任务评估指标（第4.1节）。在实验中，我们发现学习到的指标与人类评估具有高度一致性（见表2）。

items.en: We brainstorm and author 216 Creative tasks, such as “build a haunted house with zombie inside” and “race by riding a pig”. Nonetheless, such a manual approach is not scalable. Therefore, we develop two systematic approaches to extend the total number of task definitions to 1,560. This makes our Creative tasks 3 orders of magnitude larger than Minecraft BASALT challenge [104], which has 4 Creative tasks.
items.zh: 我们头脑风暴并撰写了216个创意任务，例如“建造一个有僵尸的鬼屋”和“骑猪比赛”。然而，这种手动方法不可扩展。因此，我们开发了两种系统化方法，将任务定义总数扩展到1560个。这使得我们的创意任务比Minecraft BASALT挑战[104]大三个数量级，后者只有4个创意任务。

items.en: We identify our YouTube dataset as a rich source of tasks, as many human players demonstrate and narrate creative missions in the tutorial playlists. To collect high-quality tasks and accompanying videos, we design a 3-stage pipeline that makes it easy to find and annotate interesting tasks (see Sec. C.2 for details). Through this pipeline, we extract 1,042 task ideas from the common wisdom of a huge number of veteran Minecraft gamers, such as “make an automated mining machine” and “grow cactus up to the sky”.
items.zh: 我们发现YouTube数据集是任务的丰富来源，因为许多人类玩家在教程播放列表中演示和叙述创意任务。为了收集高质量的任务和配套视频，我们设计了一个三阶段流水线，便于发现和注释有趣的任务（详见附录C.2）。通过该流水线，我们从大量资深Minecraft玩家的集体智慧中提取了1042个任务想法，例如“制造自动采矿机”和“将仙人掌种到天空”。

items.en: We leverage GPT-3’s few-shot capability to generate new task ideas by seeding it with the tasks we manually author or mine from YouTube. The prompt template is: Here are some example creative tasks in Minecraft: {a few examples}. Let’s brainstorm more detailed while reasonable creative tasks in Minecraft. GPT-3 contributes 302 creative tasks after de-duplication, and demonstrates a surprisingly proficient understanding of Minecraft terminology.
items.zh: 我们利用GPT-3的少样本能力，通过输入我们手动撰写或从YouTube挖掘的任务来生成新的任务想法。提示模板为：以下是Minecraft中的一些创意任务示例：{几个示例}。让我们头脑风暴更多详细且合理的Minecraft创意任务。GPT-3在去重后贡献了302个创意任务，并展现出对Minecraft术语令人惊讶的熟练理解。

items.en: We curate a set of 64 core tasks for future researchers to get started more easily. If their agent works well on these tasks, they can more confidently scale to the full benchmark.
items.zh: 我们策划了一组64个核心任务，以便未来研究者更容易上手。如果他们的智能体在这些任务上表现良好，他们可以更有信心地扩展到完整基准。

items.en: 32 programmatic tasks: 16 “standard” and 16 “difficult”, spanning all 4 categories (survival, harvesting, combat, and tech tree). We rely on our Minecraft knowledge to decide the difficulty level. “Standard” tasks require fewer steps and lower resource dependencies to complete.
items.zh: 32个程序化任务：16个“标准”和16个“困难”，涵盖所有4个类别（生存、采集、战斗和科技树）。我们依靠Minecraft知识来决定难度级别。“标准”任务需要更少的步骤和更低的资源依赖来完成。

items.en: 32 creative tasks: 16 “standard” and 16 “difficult”. Similarly, tasks labeled with “standard” are typically short-horizon tasks.
items.zh: 32个创意任务：16个“标准”和16个“困难”。类似地，标记为“标准”的任务通常是短视界任务。

items.en: We recommend that researchers run 100 evaluation episodes for each task and report the percentage success rate. The programmatic tasks have ground-truth success, while the creative tasks need our novel evaluation protocol (Sec. 5).
items.zh: 我们建议研究者对每个任务运行100次评估回合，并报告成功率百分比。程序化任务具有真实成功标准，而创意任务需要我们的新型评估协议（第5节）。

items.capEn: Figure 3: MineDojo’s internet-scale, multimodal knowledge base. Left, YouTube videos: Minecraft gamers showcase the impressive feats they are able to achieve. Clockwise order: an archery range, Hogwarts castle, Taj Mahal, a Nether homebase. Middle, Wiki: Wiki pages contain multimodal knowledge in structured layouts, such as comprehensive catalogs of creatures and recipes for crafting. More examples in Fig. A.4 and A.5. Right, Reddit: We create a word cloud from Reddit posts and comment threads. Gamers ask questions, share achievements, and discuss strategies extensively. Sample posts in Fig. A.7. Best viewed zoomed in.
items.capZh: 图3：MineDojo的互联网规模、多模态知识库。左，YouTube视频：Minecraft玩家展示他们能够实现的惊人壮举。顺时针顺序：射箭场、霍格沃茨城堡、泰姬陵、下界基地。中，Wiki：Wiki页面以结构化布局包含多模态知识，例如生物综合目录和合成配方。更多示例见图A.4和A.5。右，Reddit：我们从Reddit帖子和评论线程创建词云。玩家提问、分享成就并广泛讨论策略。示例帖子见图A.7。建议放大查看。

items.en: Two commonly used approaches [112, 126, 85, 36] to train embodied agents include training agents from scratch using RL with well-tuned reward functions for each task, or using a large amount of human-demonstrations to bootstrap agent learning. However, crafting well-tuned reward functions is challenging or infeasible for our task suite (Sec. 2.2), and employing expert gamers to provide large amounts of demonstration data would also be costly and infeasible [126].
items.zh: 两种常用的训练具身智能体的方法[112, 126, 85, 36]包括：使用精心调整的奖励函数为每个任务从头开始使用强化学习训练智能体，或使用大量人类演示来引导智能体学习。然而，为我们的任务套件（第2.2节）精心设计奖励函数具有挑战性或不可行，而雇佣专家玩家提供大量演示数据也成本高昂且不可行[126]。

items.en: Instead, we turn to the open web as an ever-growing, virtually unlimited source of learning material for embodied agents. The internet provides a vast amount of domain knowledge about Minecraft, which we harvest by extensive web scraping and filtering. We collect 33 years worth of YouTube videos, 6K+ Wiki pages, and millions of Reddit comment threads. Instead of hiring a handful of human demonstrators, we capture the collective wisdom of millions of Minecraft gamers around the world. Furthermore, language is a key and pervasive component of our database that takes the form of YouTube transcripts, textual descriptions in Wiki, and Reddit discussions. Language facilitates open-vocabulary understanding, provides grounding for image and video modalities, and unlocks the power of large language models [27, 109, 15] for embodied agents. To ensure socially responsible model development, we take special measures to filter out low-quality and toxic contents [13, 51] from our databases, detailed in the Appendix (Sec. D).
items.zh: 相反，我们转向开放网络，作为具身智能体不断增长、几乎无限的学习材料来源。互联网提供了大量关于Minecraft的领域知识，我们通过广泛的网络抓取和过滤来收集这些知识。我们收集了相当于33年的YouTube视频、6000多个Wiki页面和数百万条Reddit评论线程。我们没有雇佣少数人类演示者，而是捕捉了全球数百万Minecraft玩家的集体智慧。此外，语言是我们数据库的关键且普遍组成部分，以YouTube转录、Wiki文本描述和Reddit讨论的形式存在。语言促进了开放词汇理解，为图像和视频模态提供了基础，并释放了大型语言模型[27, 109, 15]在具身智能体中的潜力。为了确保社会负责任的模型开发，我们采取了特殊措施从数据库中过滤低质量和有害内容[13, 51]，详见附录（第D节）。

items.en: Minecraft is among the most streamed games on YouTube [41]. Human players have demonstrated a stunning range of creative activities and sophisticated missions that take hours to complete (examples in Fig. 3). We collect 730K+ narrated Minecraft videos, which add up to 33 years of duration and 2.2B words in English transcripts. In comparison, HowTo100M [77] is a large-scale human instructional video dataset that includes 15 years of experience in total – about half of our volume. The time-aligned transcripts enable the agent to ground free-form natural language in video pixels and learn the semantics of diverse activities without laborious human labeling. We operationalize this insight in our pre-trained video-language model (Sec. 4.1).
items.zh: Minecraft是YouTube上最常直播的游戏之一[41]。人类玩家展示了令人惊叹的创意活动和需要数小时完成的复杂任务（图3中的示例）。我们收集了超过73万个带旁白的Minecraft视频，总时长相当于33年，英语转录文本达22亿词。相比之下，HowTo100M[77]是一个大规模人类教学视频数据集，总时长约15年——约为我们的一半。时间对齐的转录使智能体能够将自由形式的自然语言与视频像素对应起来，并学习多样化活动的语义，而无需费力的人工标注。我们在预训练的视频-语言模型（第4.1节）中实现了这一见解。

items.en: The Wiki pages cover almost every aspect of the game mechanics, and supply a rich source of unstructured knowledge in multimodal tables, recipes, illustrations, and step-by-step tutorials. We use Selenium [103] to scrape 6,735 pages that interleave text, images, tables, and diagrams. The pages are highly unstructured and do not share any common schema, as the Wiki is meant for human consumption rather than AI training. To preserve the layout information, we additionally save the screenshots of entire pages and extract 2.2M bounding boxes of the visual elements (visualization in Fig. A.4 and A.5). We do not use Wiki data in our current experiments. Since the Wiki contains detailed recipes for all crafted objects, they could be provided as input or training data for hierarchical planning methods and policy sketches [8]. Another promising future direction is to apply document understanding models such as LayoutLM [138, 137] and DocFormer [9] to learn actionable knowledge from these unstructured Wiki data.
items.zh: Wiki页面几乎涵盖了游戏机制的每个方面，并以多模态表格、配方、插图和逐步教程的形式提供了丰富的非结构化知识。我们使用Selenium[103]抓取了6735个页面，这些页面交织着文本、图像、表格和图表。这些页面高度非结构化，没有共享任何通用模式，因为Wiki是为人类消费而非AI训练设计的。为了保留布局信息，我们还保存了完整页面的截图，并提取了220万个视觉元素的边界框（可视化见图A.4和A.5）。我们在当前实验中未使用Wiki数据。由于Wiki包含所有可合成物体的详细配方，它们可以作为输入或训练数据提供给分层规划方法和策略草图[8]。另一个有前景的未来方向是应用文档理解模型（如LayoutLM[138, 137]和DocFormer[9]）从这些非结构化Wiki数据中学习可操作知识。

items.en: We scrape 340K+ posts along with 6.6M comments under the “r/Minecraft” subreddit. These posts ask questions on how to solve certain tasks, showcase cool architectures and achievements in image/video snippets, and discuss general tips and tricks for players of all expertise levels. We do not use Reddit data for training in Sec. 5, but a potential idea is to finetune large language models [27, 91] on our Reddit corpus to generate instructions and execution plans that are better grounded in the Minecraft domain. Concurrent works [3, 56, 143] have explored similar ideas and showed excellent results on robot learning, which is encouraging for more future research in MineDojo.
items.zh: 我们从“r/Minecraft”子版块抓取了超过34万个帖子及其660万条评论。这些帖子询问如何解决某些任务，以图像/视频片段展示酷炫的建筑和成就，并讨论适用于所有技能水平玩家的通用技巧和窍门。我们在第5节的训练中未使用Reddit数据，但一个潜在的想法是在我们的Reddit语料库上微调大型语言模型[27, 91]，以生成更贴合Minecraft领域的指令和执行计划。并行工作[3, 56, 143]探索了类似想法，并在机器人学习上展示了出色结果，这对MineDojo的未来研究是鼓舞人心的。

full.secZh: 4 大规模预训练的智能体学习

items.capEn: Figure 4: Algorithm design. MineCLIP is a contrastive video-language model pre-trained on MineDojo’s massive Youtube database. It computes the correlation between an open-vocabulary language goal string and a 16-frame video snippet. The correlation score can be used as a learned dense reward function to train a strong multi-task RL agent.
items.capZh: 图4：算法设计。MineCLIP是一个对比性视频-语言模型，在MineDojo的大规模YouTube数据库上预训练。它计算开放词汇语言目标字符串与16帧视频片段之间的相关性。相关性得分可用作学习到的密集奖励函数，以训练强大的多任务强化学习智能体。

items.en: One of the grand challenges of embodied AI is to build a single agent that can complete a wide range of open-world tasks. The MineDojo framework aims to facilitate new techniques towards this goal by providing an open-ended task suite (Sec. 2) and large-scale internet knowledge base (Sec. 3). Here we take an initial step towards this goal by developing a proof of concept that demonstrates how a single language-prompted agent can be trained in MineDojo to complete several complex Minecraft tasks. To this end, we propose a novel agent learning algorithm that takes advantage of the massive YouTube data offered by MineDojo. We note that this is only one of the numerous possible ways to use MineDojo’s internet database — the Wiki and Reddit corpus also hold great potential to drive new algorithm discoveries for the community in future works.
items.zh: 具身AI的一大挑战是构建一个能够完成广泛开放世界任务的单一智能体。MineDojo框架旨在通过提供开放式任务套件（第2节）和大规模互联网知识库（第3节）来促进实现这一目标的新技术。在这里，我们朝着这个目标迈出了初步一步，开发了一个概念验证，展示了如何在MineDojo中训练一个单一语言提示智能体来完成几个复杂的Minecraft任务。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的智能体学习算法，利用了MineDojo提供的大量YouTube数据。我们指出，这只是使用MineDojo互联网数据库的众多可能方式之一——Wiki和Reddit语料库也蕴含着巨大潜力，可在未来工作中推动社区的新算法发现。

items.en: In this paper, we consider a multi-task reinforcement learning (RL) setting, where an agent is tasked with completing a collection of MineDojo tasks specified by language instructions (Sec. 2). Solving these tasks often requires the agent to interact with the Minecraft world in a prolonged fashion. Agents developed in popular RL benchmarks [119, 146] often rely on meticulously crafted dense and task-specific reward functions to guide random explorations. However, these rewards are hard or even infeasible to define for our diverse and open-ended tasks in MineDojo. To address this challenge, our key insight is to learn a dense, language-conditioned reward function from in-the-wild YouTube videos and their transcripts. Therefore, we introduce MineCLIP, a contrastive video-language model that learns to correlate video snippets and natural language descriptions (Fig. 4). MineCLIP is multi-task by design, as it is trained on open-vocabulary and diverse English transcripts.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们考虑一个多任务强化学习（RL）设置，其中智能体被要求完成一组由语言指令指定的MineDojo任务（第2节）。解决这些任务通常需要智能体长时间与Minecraft世界交互。在流行的RL基准[119, 146]中开发的智能体通常依赖精心设计的密集且任务特定的奖励函数来指导随机探索。然而，对于MineDojo中多样且开放式的任务，这些奖励很难甚至不可能定义。为了应对这一挑战，我们的关键见解是从野外YouTube视频及其转录中学习一个密集的、语言条件的奖励函数。因此，我们引入了MineCLIP，一个对比性视频-语言模型，学习关联视频片段和自然语言描述（图4）。MineCLIP本质上是多任务的，因为它是在开放词汇和多样化的英语转录上训练的。

items.en: During RL training, MineCLIP provides a high-quality reward signal without any domain adaptation techniques, despite the domain gap between noisy YouTube videos and clean simulator-rendered frames. MineCLIP eliminates the need to manually engineer reward functions for each and every MineDojo task. For Creative tasks that lack a simple success criterion (Sec. 2.2), MineCLIP also serves the dual purpose of an automatic evaluation metric that agrees well with human judgement on a subset of tasks we investigate (Sec. 4.2, Table 2). Because the learned reward model incurs a non-trivial computational overhead, we introduce several techniques to significantly improve RL training efficiency, making MineCLIP a practical module for open-ended agent learning in Minecraft (Sec. 4.2).
items.zh: 在RL训练期间，尽管嘈杂的YouTube视频与干净的模拟器渲染帧之间存在领域差距，MineCLIP无需任何领域自适应技术即可提供高质量的奖励信号。MineCLIP消除了为每个MineDojo任务手动设计奖励函数的需要。对于缺乏简单成功标准的创意任务（第2.2节），MineCLIP还充当自动评估指标的双重角色，在我们研究的一个子集任务上，其评估结果与人类判断高度一致（第4.2节，表2）。由于学习到的奖励模型会带来非平凡的计算开销，我们引入了多种技术来显著提高RL训练效率，使MineCLIP成为Minecraft中开放式智能体学习的实用模块（第4.2节）。

full.secZh: 4.1 在大规模视频上预训练MineCLIP

items.en: Formally, the learned reward function can be defined as Φℛ:(G,V)→ℝ:subscriptΦℛ→𝐺𝑉ℝ\Phi_{\mathcal{R}}:(G,V)\rightarrow\mathbb{R} that maps a language goal G𝐺G and a video snippet V𝑉V to a scalar reward. An ideal ΦℛsubscriptΦℛ\Phi_{\mathcal{R}} should return a high reward if the behavior depicted in the video faithfully follows the language description, and a low reward otherwise. This can be achieved by optimizing the InfoNCE objective [125, 52, 20], which learns to correlate positive video and text pairs [118, 6, 78, 4, 136].
items.zh: 形式上，学习到的奖励函数可以定义为Φℛ:(G,V)→ℝ，将语言目标G和视频片段V映射到标量奖励。理想的Φℛ应该在视频中展示的行为忠实遵循语言描述时返回高奖励，否则返回低奖励。这可以通过优化InfoNCE目标[125, 52, 20]来实现，该目标学习关联正例视频和文本对[118, 6, 78, 4, 136]。

items.en: Similar to the image-text CLIP model [92], MineCLIP is composed of a separate text encoder ϕGsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐺\phi_{G} that embeds a language goal and a video encoder ϕVsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑉\phi_{V} that embeds a moving window of 16 consecutive frames with 160×256160256160\times 256 resolution (Fig. 4). Our neural architecture has a similar design as CLIP4Clip [75], where ϕGsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐺\phi_{G} reuses OpenAI CLIP’s pretrained text encoder, and ϕVsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑉\phi_{V} is factorized into a frame-wise image encoder ϕIsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐼\phi_{I} and a temporal aggregator ϕasubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑎\phi_{a} that summarizes the sequence of 16 image features into a single video embedding. Unlike CLIP4Clip, we insert two extra layers of residual CLIP Adapter [38] after the aggregator ϕasubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑎\phi_{a} to produce a better video feature, and finetune only the last two layers of the pretrained ϕIsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐼\phi_{I} and ϕGsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐺\phi_{G}.
items.zh: 类似于图像-文本CLIP模型[92]，MineCLIP由一个独立的文本编码器ϕG（嵌入语言目标）和一个视频编码器ϕV（嵌入一个包含16帧的滑动窗口，分辨率为160×256）组成（图4）。我们的神经架构与CLIP4Clip[75]设计类似，其中ϕG重用OpenAI CLIP的预训练文本编码器，而ϕV分解为逐帧图像编码器ϕI和时间聚合器ϕa，后者将16个图像特征序列汇总为单个视频嵌入。与CLIP4Clip不同，我们在聚合器ϕa之后插入两个额外的残差CLIP适配器[38]层以产生更好的视频特征，并且仅微调预训练ϕI和ϕG的最后两层。

items.en: From the MineDojo YouTube database, we follow the procedure in VideoCLIP [136] to sample 640K pairs of 16-second video snippets and time-aligned English transcripts, after applying a keyword filter. We train two MineCLIP variants with different types of aggregator ϕasubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑎\phi_{a}: (1) MineCLIP[avg] does simple average pooling, which is fast but agnostic to the temporal ordering; (2) MineCLIP[attn] encodes the sequence by two transformer layers, which is relatively slower but captures more temporal information, and thus produces a better reward signal in general. Details of data preprocessing, architecture, and hyperparameters are listed in the Appendix (Sec. E).
items.zh: 从MineDojo YouTube数据库中，我们遵循VideoCLIP[136]中的程序，在应用关键词过滤器后，采样了64万个16秒视频片段和时间对齐的英语转录对。我们训练了两种MineCLIP变体，使用不同类型的聚合器ϕa：（1）MineCLIP[avg]进行简单的平均池化，速度快但对时间顺序不敏感；（2）MineCLIP[attn]通过两个Transformer层编码序列，相对较慢但捕获更多时间信息，因此通常产生更好的奖励信号。数据预处理、架构和超参数的详细信息见附录（第E节）。

items.en: We train a language-conditioned policy network that takes as input raw pixels and predicts discrete control. The policy is trained with PPO [102] on the MineCLIP rewards. In each episode, the agent is prompted with a language goal and takes a sequence of actions to fulfill this goal. When calculating the MineCLIP rewards, we concatenate the agent’s latest 16 egocentric RGB frames in a temporal window to form a video snippet. MineCLIP handles all task prompts zero-shot without any further finetuning. In our experiments (Sec. 5), we show that MineCLIP provides effective dense rewards out of the box, despite the domain shift between in-the-wild YouTube frames and simulator frames. Besides regular video data augmentation, we do not employ any special domain adaptation methods during pre-training. Our finding is consistent with CLIP’s strong zero-shot performances on robustness benchmarks in object recognition [92].
items.zh: 我们训练了一个语言条件策略网络，该网络以原始像素为输入并预测离散控制。该策略使用PPO [102]在MineCLIP奖励上进行训练。在每个回合中，智能体被提示一个语言目标，并采取一系列动作来实现该目标。在计算MineCLIP奖励时，我们将智能体最新的16个第一人称RGB帧在时间窗口内拼接成一个视频片段。MineCLIP以零样本方式处理所有任务提示，无需进一步微调。在我们的实验（第5节）中，我们展示了MineCLIP开箱即用地提供了有效的密集奖励，尽管野外YouTube帧与模拟器帧之间存在域偏移。除了常规的视频数据增强外，我们在预训练期间没有采用任何特殊的域适应方法。我们的发现与CLIP在目标识别鲁棒性基准测试中的强零样本性能一致[92]。

items.en: Compared to hard-coded reward functions in popular benchmarks [146, 119, 34], the MineCLIP model has 150M parameters and is thus much more expensive to query. We make several design choices to greatly accelerate RL training with MineCLIP in the loop:
items.zh: 与流行基准测试中硬编码的奖励函数[146, 119, 34]相比，MineCLIP模型有1.5亿参数，因此查询成本更高。我们做出了几个设计选择，以大大加速带有MineCLIP的强化学习训练：

items.en: The language goal G𝐺G is fixed for a specific task, so the text features ϕGsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐺\phi_{G} can be precomputed to avoid invoking the text encoder repeatedly.
items.zh: 语言目标G对于特定任务是固定的，因此文本特征φG可以预先计算，以避免重复调用文本编码器。

items.en: Our agent’s RGB encoder reuses the pre-trained weights of ϕIsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐼\phi_{I} from MineCLIP. We do not finetune ϕIsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐼\phi_{I} during RL training, which saves computation and endows the agent with good visual representations from the beginning.
items.zh: 我们智能体的RGB编码器重用了MineCLIP中φI的预训练权重。在强化学习训练期间，我们不微调φI，这节省了计算量，并使智能体从一开始就拥有良好的视觉表示。

items.en: MineCLIP’s video encoder ϕVsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑉\phi_{V} is factorized into an image encoder ϕIsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝐼\phi_{I} and a light-weight aggregator ϕasubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑎\phi_{a}. This design choice enables efficient image feature caching. Consider two overlapping video sequences of 8 frames, V[0:8] and V[1:9]. We can cache the image features of the 7 overlapping frames V[1] to V[7] to maximize compute savings. If ϕVsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑉\phi_{V} is a monolithic model like S3D [135] in VideoCLIP [136], then the video features from every sliding window must be recomputed, which would incur a much higher cost per time step.
items.zh: MineCLIP的视频编码器φV被分解为一个图像编码器φI和一个轻量级聚合器φa。这种设计选择实现了高效的图像特征缓存。考虑两个重叠的8帧视频序列V[0:8]和V[1:9]。我们可以缓存7个重叠帧V[1]到V[7]的图像特征，以最大化计算节省。如果φV是一个像VideoCLIP [136]中的S3D [135]那样的整体模型，那么每个滑动窗口的视频特征都必须重新计算，这将导致每个时间步的成本高得多。

items.en: We leverage Self-Imitation Learning [84] to store the trajectories with high MineCLIP reward values in a buffer, and alternate between PPO and self-imitation gradient steps. It further improves sample efficiency as shown in the Appendix (Fig. A.8).
items.zh: 我们利用自模仿学习[84]将具有高MineCLIP奖励值的轨迹存储在缓冲区中，并在PPO和自模仿梯度步骤之间交替进行。这进一步提高了样本效率，如附录（图A.8）所示。

items.en: We evaluate our agent-learning approach (Section 4) on 8 Programmatic tasks and 4 Creative tasks from the MineDojo benchmarking suite. We select these 12 tasks due to the diversity of skills required to solve them (e.g., harvesting, combat, building, navigation) and domain-specific entities (e.g., animals, resources, monsters, terrains, and structures). We split the tasks into 3 groups and train one multi-task agent for each group: Animal-Zoo (4 Programmatic tasks on hunting or harvesting resource from animals), Mob-Combat (Programmatic, fight 4 types of hostile monsters), and Creative (4 tasks).
items.zh: 我们在MineDojo基准测试套件的8个程序化任务和4个创意任务上评估了我们的智能体学习方法（第4节）。我们选择这12个任务是因为解决它们所需的技能多样性（例如，收获、战斗、建造、导航）和领域特定实体（例如，动物、资源、怪物、地形和结构）。我们将任务分为3组，并为每组训练一个多任务智能体：动物动物园（4个关于狩猎或从动物收获资源的程序化任务）、怪物战斗（程序化，对抗4种敌对怪物）和创意（4个任务）。

items.en: In the experiments, we empirically check the quality of MineCLIP against manually written reward functions, and quantify how different variants of our learned model affect the RL performance. Table 1 presents our main results, and Fig. 2 visualizes our learned agent behavior in 4 of the considered tasks. Policy networks of all methods share the same architecture and are trained by PPO + Self-Imitation (Sec. 4.2, training details in the Appendix, Sec. F). We compare the following methods:
items.zh: 在实验中，我们经验性地检查了MineCLIP与手动编写的奖励函数的质量，并量化了我们学习模型的不同变体如何影响强化学习性能。表1展示了我们的主要结果，图2可视化了我们在4个考虑任务中学习到的智能体行为。所有方法的策略网络共享相同的架构，并通过PPO + 自模仿（第4.2节，训练细节见附录第F节）进行训练。我们比较了以下方法：

items.en: Ours (Attn): our agent trained with the MineCLIP[attn] reward model. For Programmatic tasks, we also add the final success condition as a binary reward. For Creative tasks, MineCLIP is the only source of reward.
items.zh: 我们的方法（注意力）：我们的智能体使用MineCLIP[注意力]奖励模型进行训练。对于程序化任务，我们还添加了最终成功条件作为二元奖励。对于创意任务，MineCLIP是唯一的奖励来源。

items.en: Ours (Avg): the average-pooling variant of our method.
items.zh: 我们的方法（平均池化）：我们方法的平均池化变体。

items.en: Manual Reward: hand-engineered dense reward using ground-truth simulator states.
items.zh: 手动奖励：使用真实模拟器状态的手工工程密集奖励。

items.en: Sparse-only: the final binary success as a single sparse reward. Note that neither sparse-only nor manual reward is available for Creative tasks.
items.zh: 仅稀疏奖励：仅使用最终二元成功作为稀疏奖励。注意，对于创意任务，仅稀疏奖励和手动奖励都不可用。

items.en: CLIPOpenAI: pre-trained OpenAI CLIP model that has not been finetuned on any MineDojo videos.
items.zh: CLIPOpenAI：预训练的OpenAI CLIP模型，未在任何MineDojo视频上进行微调。

items.en: For Programmatic tasks (first 8 rows), RL agents guided by MineCLIP achieve competitive performance as those trained by manual reward. In three of the tasks, they even outperform the hand-engineered reward functions, which rely on privileged simulator states unavailable to MineCLIP. For a more statistically sound analysis, we conduct the Paired Student’s t-test to compare the mean success rate of each task (pairing column 3 “Ours (Attn)” and column 5 “Manual Reward” in Table 1). The test yields p-value 0.3991≫0.05much-greater-than0.39910.050.3991\gg 0.05, which indicates that the difference between our method and manual reward is not considered statistically significant, and therefore they are comparable with each other. Because all tasks require nontrivial exploration, our approach also dominates the Sparse-only baseline. Note that the original OpenAI CLIP model fails to achieve any success. We hypothesize that the creatures in Minecraft look dramatically different from their real-world counterparts, which causes CLIP to produce misleading signals worse than no shaping reward at all. It implies the importance of finetuning on MineDojo’s YouTube data.
items.zh: 对于程序化任务（前8行），由MineCLIP引导的强化学习智能体实现了与手动奖励训练的智能体相当的性能。在三个任务中，它们甚至优于依赖MineCLIP不可用的特权模拟器状态的手工工程奖励函数。为了进行更统计上可靠的分析，我们进行了配对学生t检验，比较每个任务的平均成功率（在表1中配对第3列“我们的方法（注意力）”和第5列“手动奖励”）。检验得出p值为0.3991≫0.05，这表明我们的方法与手动奖励之间的差异不被认为具有统计显著性，因此它们之间具有可比性。由于所有任务都需要非平凡的探索，我们的方法也优于仅稀疏奖励基线。注意，原始的OpenAI CLIP模型未能取得任何成功。我们假设Minecraft中的生物看起来与现实世界中的对应物截然不同，这导致CLIP产生误导性信号，比没有塑形奖励更差。这暗示了在MineDojo的YouTube数据上进行微调的重要性。

items.en: For Creative tasks (last 4 rows), there are no programmatic success criteria available. We convert MineCLIP into a binary success classifier by thresholding the reward value it outputs for an episode. To test the quality of MineCLIP as an automatic evaluation metric, we ask human judges to curate a dataset of 100 successful and 100 failed trajectories for each task. We then run both MineCLIP variants and CLIPOpenAI on the dataset and report the binary F1 score of their judgement against human ground-truth in Table 2. The results demonstrate that both MineCLIP[attn] and MineCLIP[avg] attain a very high degree of agreement with human evaluation results on this subset of the Creative task suite that we investigate. CLIPOpenAI baseline also achieves nontrivial agreement on Find Ocean and Dig Hole tasks, likely because real-world oceans and holes have similar texture. We use the attn variant as an automated success criterion to score the 4 Creative task results in Table 1. Our proposed method consistently learns better than CLIPOpenAI-guided agents. It shows that MineCLIP is an effective approach to solving open-ended tasks when no straightforward reward signal is available. We provide further analysis beyond these 4 tasks in the Appendix (Sec. G.4).
items.zh: 对于创意任务（最后4行），没有可用的程序化成功标准。我们通过阈值化MineCLIP对一个回合输出的奖励值，将其转换为二元成功分类器。为了测试MineCLIP作为自动评估指标的质量，我们请人类评委为每个任务策划了一个包含100个成功和100个失败轨迹的数据集。然后，我们在数据集上运行MineCLIP的两个变体和CLIPOpenAI，并报告它们判断与人类真实情况之间的二元F1分数，如表2所示。结果表明，MineCLIP[注意力]和MineCLIP[平均池化]在我们调查的创意任务子集上，与人类评估结果达到了非常高的一致性。CLIPOpenAI基线在“寻找海洋”和“挖洞”任务上也取得了非平凡的一致性，可能是因为现实世界的海洋和洞具有相似的纹理。我们使用注意力变体作为自动成功标准，对表1中的4个创意任务结果进行评分。我们提出的方法始终优于CLIPOpenAI引导的智能体。这表明，当没有直接的奖励信号可用时，MineCLIP是解决开放式任务的有效方法。我们在附录（第G.4节）中提供了超出这4个任务的进一步分析。

items.en: We evaluate the learned policy without finetuning on a combination of unseen weather, lighting conditions, and terrains — 27 scenarios in total. For the baseline, we train agents with the original CLIPOpenAI image encoder (not trained on our YouTube videos) by imitation learning. The robustness against visual shift can be quantitatively measured by the relative performance degradation on novel test scenarios for each task. Table 3 shows that while all methods incur performance drops, agents with MineCLIP encoder is more robust to visual changes than the baseline across all tasks. Pre-training on diverse in-the-wild YouTube videos is important to boosting zero-shot visual generalization capability, a finding consistent with literature [92, 82].
items.zh: 我们在未见过的天气、光照条件和地形的组合——总共27种场景——上评估了学习到的策略，无需微调。对于基线，我们通过模仿学习使用原始的CLIPOpenAI图像编码器（未在我们的YouTube视频上训练）训练智能体。对视觉变化的鲁棒性可以通过每个任务在新测试场景上的相对性能下降来定量衡量。表3显示，尽管所有方法都出现了性能下降，但使用MineCLIP编码器的智能体在所有任务上比基线对视觉变化更鲁棒。在多样化的野外YouTube视频上进行预训练对于提升零样本视觉泛化能力很重要，这一发现与文献[92, 82]一致。

items.en: We have also trained a single agent for all 12 tasks. To reduce the computational burden without loss of generality, the agent is trained by self-imitating from successful trajectories generated from the self-imitation learning pipeline (Section F.3). We summarize the result in Table 4. Similar to our main experiments, all numbers represent percentage success rates averaged over 3 training seeds, each tested for 200 episodes. Compared to the original agents, the new 12-multitask agent sees a performance boost in 6 tasks, degradation in 4 tasks, and roughly the same success rates in 2 tasks. This result suggests that there are both positive and negative task transfers happening. To improve the multi-task performance, more advanced algorithms [141, 133] can be employed to mitigate the negative transfer effects.
items.zh: 我们还训练了一个用于所有12个任务的单一智能体。为了在不失一般性的情况下减少计算负担，该智能体通过从自模仿学习流程（第F.3节）生成的成功轨迹进行自模仿训练。我们在表4中总结了结果。与我们的主要实验类似，所有数字代表平均超过3个训练种子的成功率百分比，每个种子测试200个回合。与原始智能体相比，新的12任务多任务智能体在6个任务上性能提升，在4个任务上性能下降，在2个任务上成功率大致相同。这一结果表明存在正负任务迁移。为了提高多任务性能，可以采用更先进的算法[141, 133]来减轻负迁移效应。

items.en: We also perform Paired Student’s t-test to statistically compare the performance of the 12-multitask agent and those separately trained on each task group. We obtain a p-value of 0.3720≫0.05much-greater-than0.37200.050.3720\gg 0.05, which suggests that the difference between the two training settings is not statistically significant.
items.zh: 我们还进行了配对学生t检验，以统计比较12任务多智能体与在每个任务组上单独训练的智能体的性能。我们得到p值为0.3720≫0.05，这表明两种训练设置之间的差异不具有统计显著性。

items.en: To test the ability to generalize to new open-vocabulary commands, we evaluate the agent on two novel tasks: “harvest spider string” and “hunt pig”. Table 5 shows that the agent struggles in the zero-shot setting because it has not interacted with pigs or spider strings during training, and thus does not know how to interact with them effectively. However, the performance improves substantially by finetuning with the MineCLIP reward. Here the baseline methods are trained from scratch using RL with the MineCLIP encoders and reward. Therefore, the only difference is whether the policy has been pre-trained on the 12 tasks or not. Given the same environment sampling budget (only around 5% of total samples), it significantly outperforms baselines. It suggests that the multitask agent has learned transferable knowledge on hunting and resource collection, which enables it to quickly adapt to novel tasks.
items.zh: 为了测试对新开放词汇命令的泛化能力，我们在两个新任务上评估了智能体：“收获蜘蛛丝”和“猎猪”。表5显示，智能体在零样本设置中表现不佳，因为它在训练期间没有与猪或蜘蛛丝交互，因此不知道如何有效地与它们交互。然而，通过使用MineCLIP奖励进行微调，性能显著提高。这里的基线方法是从头开始使用MineCLIP编码器和奖励进行强化学习训练的。因此，唯一的区别是策略是否在12个任务上进行了预训练。在相同的环境采样预算下（仅占总样本的约5%），它显著优于基线。这表明多任务智能体已经学习了关于狩猎和资源收集的可迁移知识，使其能够快速适应新任务。

items.en: There are many environments developed with the goal of open-ended agent learning. Prior works include maze-style worlds [121, 129, 61], purely text-based game [69], grid worlds [21, 16], browser/GUI-based environments [108, 124], and indoor simulators for robotics [1, 107, 114, 34, 110, 99, 89]. Minecraft offers an exciting alternative for open-ended agent learning. It is a 3D visual world with procedurally generated landscapes and extremely flexible game mechanics that support an enormous variety of activities. Prior methods in open-ended agent learning [30, 57, 130, 63, 26] do not make use of external knowledge, but our approach leverages internet-scale database to learn open-vocabulary reward models, thanks to Minecraft’s abundance of gameplay data online.
items.zh: 有许多环境是以开放式智能体学习为目标开发的。先前的工作包括迷宫风格的世界[121, 129, 61]、纯文本游戏[69]、网格世界[21, 16]、浏览器/GUI环境[108, 124]以及用于机器人的室内模拟器[1, 107, 114, 34, 110, 99, 89]。Minecraft为开放式智能体学习提供了一个令人兴奋的替代方案。它是一个3D视觉世界，具有程序生成的地形和极其灵活的游戏机制，支持各种各样的活动。先前在开放式智能体学习中的方法[30, 57, 130, 63, 26]没有利用外部知识，但我们的方法利用互联网规模的数据集来学习开放词汇奖励模型，这得益于Minecraft在线游戏数据的丰富性。

items.en: The Malmo platform [60] is the first comprehensive release of a Gym-style agent API [14] for Minecraft. Based on Malmo, MineRL [48] provides a codebase and human play trajectories for the annual Diamond Challenge at NeurIPS [47, 49, 62]. MineDojo’s simulator builds upon the pioneering work of MineRL, but greatly expands the API and benchmarking task suite. Other Minecraft benchmarks exist with different focuses. For example, CraftAssist [44] and IGLU [66] study agents with interactive dialogues. BASALT [104] applies human evaluation to 4 open-ended tasks. EvoCraft [45] is designed for structure building, and Crafter [50] optimizes for fast experimentation. Unlike prior works, MineDojo’s core mission is to facilitate the development of generally capable embodied agents using internet-scale knowledge. We include a feature comparison table of different Minecraft platforms for AI research in Table A.1.
items.zh: Malmo平台[60]是首个全面发布的Minecraft Gym风格智能体API[14]。基于Malmo，MineRL[48]为NeurIPS年度钻石挑战赛提供了代码库和人类游戏轨迹[47, 49, 62]。MineDojo的模拟器建立在MineRL的开创性工作之上，但大幅扩展了API和基准测试任务套件。其他Minecraft基准测试具有不同的侧重点。例如，CraftAssist[44]和IGLU[66]研究具有交互式对话的智能体。BASALT[104]将人类评估应用于4个开放式任务。EvoCraft[45]专为结构构建而设计，Crafter[50]则针对快速实验进行了优化。与先前工作不同，MineDojo的核心使命是利用互联网规模的知识促进通用具身智能体的开发。我们在表A.1中包含了不同Minecraft AI研究平台的特征对比表。

items.en: Big dataset such as Common Crawl [24], the Pile [37], LAION [100], YouTube-8M [2] and HowTo100M [77] have been fueling the success of large pre-trained language models [27, 91, 15] and multimodal models [118, 6, 78, 145, 7, 4, 136]. While generally useful for learning representations, these datasets are not specifically targeted at embodied agents. To provide agent-centric training data, RoboNet [25] collects video frames from 7 robot platforms, and Ego4D [43] recruits volunteers to record egocentric videos of household activities. In comparison, MineDojo’s knowledge base is constructed without human curation efforts, much larger in volume, more diverse in data modalities, and comprehensively covers all aspects of the Minecraft environment.
items.zh: 诸如Common Crawl[24]、the Pile[37]、LAION[100]、YouTube-8M[2]和HowTo100M[77]等大型数据集一直推动着大型预训练语言模型[27, 91, 15]和多模态模型[118, 6, 78, 145, 7, 4, 136]的成功。虽然这些数据集通常有助于学习表示，但它们并非专门针对具身智能体。为了提供以智能体为中心的训练数据，RoboNet[25]从7个机器人平台收集视频帧，Ego4D[43]招募志愿者录制家庭活动的第一人称视频。相比之下，MineDojo的知识库无需人工整理，数据量更大，数据模态更多样，并全面覆盖了Minecraft环境的各个方面。

items.en: Inspired by the success in NLP, embodied agent research [29, 11, 94, 23] has seen a surge in adoption of the large-scale pre-training paradigm. The recent advances can be roughly divided into 4 categories. 1) Novel agent architecture: Decision Transformer [19, 58, 144] applies the powerful self-attention models to sequential decision making. GATO [95] and Unified-IO [74] learn a single model to solve various decision-making tasks with different control interfaces. VIMA [59] unifies a wide range of robot manipulation tasks with multimodal prompting. 2) Pre-training for better representations: R3M [82] trains a general-purpose visual encoder for robot perception on Ego4D videos [43]. CLIPort [111] leverages the pre-trained CLIP model [92] to enable free-form language instructions for robot manipulation. 3) Pre-training for better policies: AlphaStar [126] achieves champion-level performance on StarCraft by imitating from numerous human demos. SayCan [3] leverages large language models (LMs) to ground value functions in the physical world. [72] and [96] directly reuse pre-trained LMs as policy backbone. VPT [10] is a concurrent work that learns an inverse dynamics model from human contractors to pseudo-label YouTube videos for behavior cloning. VPT is complementary to our approach, and can be finetuned to solve language-conditioned open-ended tasks with our learned reward model. 4) Data-driven reward functions: Concept2Robot [105] and DVD [18] learn a binary classifier to score behaviors from in-the-wild videos [42]. LOReL [81] crowd-sources humans labels to train language-conditioned reward function for offline RL. AVID [113] and XIRL [142] extract reward signals via cycle consistency. MineDojo’s task benchmark and internet knowledge base are generally useful for developing new algorithms in all the above categories. In Sec. 4, we also propose an open-vocabulary, multi-task reward model using MineDojo YouTube videos.
items.zh: 受NLP成功的启发，具身智能体研究[29, 11, 94, 23]越来越多地采用大规模预训练范式。最近的进展大致可分为4类。1) 新颖的智能体架构：Decision Transformer[19, 58, 144]将强大的自注意力模型应用于序列决策。GATO[95]和Unified-IO[74]学习单一模型以不同的控制接口解决各种决策任务。VIMA[59]通过多模态提示统一了广泛的机器人操作任务。2) 预训练以获得更好的表示：R3M[82]在Ego4D视频[43]上训练通用视觉编码器用于机器人感知。CLIPort[111]利用预训练的CLIP模型[92]实现机器人操作的自由形式语言指令。3) 预训练以获得更好的策略：AlphaStar[126]通过模仿大量人类演示在星际争霸中达到冠军级表现。SayCan[3]利用大型语言模型（LM）将价值函数锚定在物理世界中。[72]和[96]直接重用预训练的LM作为策略主干。VPT[10]是一项并行工作，它从人类承包商那里学习逆动力学模型，为YouTube视频伪标签以进行行为克隆。VPT与我们的方法互补，并且可以通过我们学习的奖励模型进行微调以解决语言条件的开放式任务。4) 数据驱动的奖励函数：Concept2Robot[105]和DVD[18]学习二元分类器从野外视频[42]中评分行为。LOReL[81]众包人类标签来训练语言条件的奖励函数用于离线强化学习。AVID[113]和XIRL[142]通过循环一致性提取奖励信号。MineDojo的任务基准和互联网知识库对于在上述所有类别中开发新算法普遍有用。在第4节中，我们还提出了一种使用MineDojo YouTube视频的开放词汇、多任务奖励模型。

items.en: In this work, we introduce the MineDojo framework for developing generally capable embodied agents. MineDojo features a benchmarking suite of thousands of Programmatic and Creative tasks, and an internet-scale multimodal knowledge base of videos, wiki, and forum discussions. As an example of the novel research possibilities enabled by MineDojo, we propose MineCLIP as an effective language-conditioned reward function trained with in-the-wild YouTube videos. MineCLIP achieves strong performance empirically and agrees well with human evaluation results, making it a good automatic metric for Creative tasks. We look forward to seeing how MineDojo empowers the community to make progress on the important challenge of open-ended agent learning.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们介绍了用于开发通用具身智能体的MineDojo框架。MineDojo包含一个包含数千个程序化和创造性任务的基准测试套件，以及一个包含视频、维基和论坛讨论的互联网规模多模态知识库。作为MineDojo所实现的新研究可能性的一个例子，我们提出了MineCLIP，这是一种使用野外YouTube视频训练的有效语言条件奖励函数。MineCLIP在经验上表现出色，并且与人类评估结果高度一致，使其成为创造性任务的良好自动评估指标。我们期待看到MineDojo如何赋能社区在开放式智能体学习这一重要挑战上取得进展。
